title: Chicken Karahi

seo.title: Authentic Chicken Karahi Recipe | Pakistani Lunch Dish
seo.metaDescription: Master the art of authentic Pakistani Chicken Karahi with our easy-to-follow recipe. Perfect for lunch or dinner, this spicy, flavorful dish will be a family favorite!
h2Headings: How to Make Chicken Karahi: Step-by-Step
Pro Tips for the Best Chicken Karahi
Variations and Substitutions for Chicken Karahi
Frequently Asked Questions about Chicken Karahi
seo.imageAlt: A vibrant, authentic Pakistani Chicken Karahi served in a traditional karahi pot, garnished with ginger and green chilies.
seo.imageCaption: Indulge in the rich, spicy flavors of this traditional Pakistani Chicken Karahi, a perfect dish for any occasion.

description: Chicken Karahi is a beloved Pakistani culinary masterpiece, known for its rich, spicy, and tangy flavors. This dish features tender chicken pieces cooked to perfection in a wok-like karahi with fresh tomatoes, ginger, garlic, and a blend of aromatic spices. It's a truly irresistible and satisfying meal, perfect for sharing with family and friends.

instructions.title: Sear the Chicken
instructions.text: Heat cooking oil in a karahi or large wok over high heat. Add the chicken pieces and fry until they change color and are lightly browned on all sides, which usually takes about 5-7 minutes.

instructions.title: Add Aromatics and Tomatoes
instructions.text: Stir in the ginger-garlic paste and fry for another 2 minutes until fragrant. Then, add the chopped or pureed tomatoes, salt, red chili powder, turmeric powder, coriander powder, and cumin powder. Mix all ingredients thoroughly.

instructions.title: Cook Down the Gravy
instructions.text: Cover the karahi and cook on medium heat for 15-20 minutes, or until the tomatoes have softened and completely broken down, and the oil begins to separate from the masala. Stir occasionally to prevent sticking.

instructions.title: Incorporate Yogurt and Chilies
instructions.text: If using, add the whisked yogurt and stir continuously for 2-3 minutes to prevent curdling. Add the slit green chilies and continue to cook on medium-high heat, stirring until the oil fully separates and the gravy thickens to your desired consistency.

instructions.title: Simmer and Finish
instructions.text: Reduce the heat to low, cover the karahi, and let it simmer for another 5-7 minutes. This allows the flavors to meld beautifully and ensures the chicken becomes exceptionally tender.

instructions.title: Garnish and Serve
instructions.text: Finally, sprinkle with garam masala powder, julienned fresh ginger, and chopped fresh coriander. Serve the Chicken Karahi hot with fresh naan, roti, or rice for a complete and satisfying meal.

tips: High Heat for Flavor: Start cooking the chicken on high heat to sear it quickly and lock in the juices, contributing to a richer flavor profile, a hallmark of authentic karahi.
Fresh Tomatoes are Key: Using fresh, ripe tomatoes is crucial for the authentic tang and texture of the karahi gravy. Avoid tomato paste or canned tomatoes if possible for the best results.
Serve Immediately: Chicken Karahi is best enjoyed fresh off the stove. Serve it hot with warm naan, roti, or chapati to fully appreciate its vibrant flavors and tender chicken.
article: Chicken Karahi is a beloved Pakistani Lunch Recipes dish enjoyed across Pakistan. This authentic recipe brings traditional flavors to your kitchen.
